blocks.text: UNIVERSITY TIMETABLE OPTIMIZATION SYSTEM
(UTOS)
Operation Contracts
UC00 through UC22  ·  23 System Operations
By: Muhammad Hammad Shakeel
Software Engineering Project  ·  May 2026
About This Document

This document provides a formal Operation Contract for every system operation identified in the UTOS System Sequence Diagrams (SSDs). Each contract follows the standard template used in Object-Oriented Analysis, specifying the operation signature, responsible use case, participating actors, cross-references, preconditions, postconditions (state changes), output/return values, and notable exceptions.
Template Fields
Operation: Full signature with parameters and types.
Use Case: The SSD / use case from which the operation is derived.
Actors: Who initiates or participates in the operation.
Cross References: Other use cases or operations related to this contract.
Notes: Implementation notes or special considerations.
Exceptions: Failure scenarios and system responses.
Output / Return: Data returned to the calling actor or system.
Pre-conditions: Conditions that must be true before the operation executes.
Post-conditions: State changes guaranteed upon successful completion (past-tense assertions).
OC-00 — UC00 — Authenticate and Login
Operation:  authenticateUser(userId: String, password: String, mfaToken: String [optional])
Use Case:  UC00 — Authenticate and Login
Actors:  All Users, System Administrator
Cross References:  UC21 (Manage Users and Roles)
Notes:  Passwords are stored as salted hashes. MFA token is optional but enforced for Administrator accounts.
Exceptions:  Invalid credentials → error message displayed; attempt logged. Account locked → locked-account message displayed. MFA failure → authentication rejected.
Output:  Session token; user role and permissions profile; redirect URL for the user's home dashboard.
Pre-conditions:
▪  The system is online and the login page is accessible.
▪  The user account exists in the system.
Post-conditions:
▪  A validated session token was created and associated with the user.
▪  The session was logged in the audit trail.
▪  The user's last-login timestamp was updated.
OC-01 — UC01 — Maintain Organization Hierarchy
Operation:  maintainOrganizationHierarchy(action: Enum{ADD, UPDATE, DELETE}, nodeData: OrgNode)
Use Case:  UC01 — Maintain Organization Hierarchy
Actors:  Timetable Administrator
Cross References:  UC03 (Manage Master Data), UC21 (Manage Users and Roles)
Notes:  Nil
Exceptions:  Duplicate node name at same level → validation error. DELETE with active timetable references → operation rejected with conflict list.
Output:  Updated organization hierarchy tree; confirmation message with node ID.
Pre-conditions:
▪  Administrator is authenticated and has ADMIN role.
▪  For UPDATE/DELETE: the target organizational node exists.
Post-conditions:
▪  For ADD: a new organizational node (faculty/department/program) was persisted.
▪  For UPDATE: node attributes were updated in the database.
▪  For DELETE: node and its children were removed and referential integrity was maintained.
▪  Change event was recorded in the audit log.
OC-02 — UC02 — Maintain Academic Calendar
Operation:  maintainAcademicCalendar(action: Enum{CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE}, calendarData: CalendarPeriod)
Use Case:  UC02 — Maintain Academic Calendar
Actors:  Timetable Administrator
Cross References:  UC05 (Generate Class Timetable), UC19 (Schedule Exams)
Notes:  Nil
Exceptions:  Overlapping date ranges → validation error with conflict details. DELETE of period with published timetable → rejected with advisory message.
Output:  Persisted academic calendar record; generated list of teaching weeks and blackout dates.
Pre-conditions:
▪  Administrator is authenticated.
▪  For UPDATE/DELETE: the target academic period exists and is not currently active for timetabling.
Post-conditions:
▪  Calendar period (semester, term, holidays) was created/updated/deleted in the system.
▪  Teaching weeks and exam windows were recalculated if dates changed.
▪  Audit log entry was created.
OC-03 — UC03 — Manage Master Data
Operation:  manageMasterData(entityType: Enum{ROOM, COURSE, TEACHER, SECTION, ...}, action: Enum{ADD, UPDATE, DELETE}, entityData: Object)
Use Case:  UC03 — Manage Master Data
Actors:  Timetable Administrator, Department Coordinator, Facility Manager
Cross References:  UC04, UC05, UC16, UC17, UC18, UC19
Notes:  Nil

Exceptions:  Duplicate entity key → rejected with duplicate-record message. DELETE of entity referenced in active timetable → rejected with dependency list. Invalid capacity or attribute value → field-level validation error.
Output:  Persisted entity record with system-generated ID; updated data catalogue entry.
Pre-conditions:
▪  Actor is authenticated with appropriate data-management permissions.
▪  For UPDATE/DELETE: the target entity record exists.
Post-conditions:
▪  Master data entity (room, course, teacher, section, etc.) was created/updated/deleted.
▪  Related constraint records were updated if capacity or attributes changed.
▪  Change was logged in the audit trail.
OC-04 — UC04 — Define Constraints and Preferences
Operation:  defineConstraint(constraintSpec: ConstraintDTO, priority: Enum{HARD, SOFT}, scope: Enum{GLOBAL, DEPARTMENT, TEACHER})
Use Case:  UC04 — Define Constraints and Preferences
Actors:  Timetable Administrator, Department Coordinator, Teacher
Cross References:  UC05 (Generate Class Timetable), UC10 (Re-optimize Timetable)
Notes:  Nil
Exceptions:  Conflicting HARD constraints → system flags potential infeasibility and warns actor. Permission violation (e.g., Teacher sets global constraint) → rejected.
Output:  Constraint ID; constraint summary with priority and scope; validation result (feasibility check).
Pre-conditions:
▪  Actor is authenticated with constraint-definition permissions.
▪  Referenced entities (rooms, teachers, time slots) exist in master data.
Post-conditions:
▪  Constraint or preference record was persisted and linked to the relevant scope.
▪  Constraint was marked as ACTIVE and will be included in the next solver run.
▪  Audit entry was created with actor identity.
OC-05 — UC05 — Generate Class Timetable
Operation:  generateClassTimetable(semesterId: String, configParams: SolverConfig)
Use Case:  UC05 — Generate Class Timetable
Actors:  Timetable Administrator, Solver Engine
Cross References:  UC04 (Constraints), UC06 (Review Quality), UC10 (Re-optimize)
Notes:  Nil

Exceptions:  HARD constraint infeasibility → generation fails; infeasibility report returned. Solver timeout → partial result saved with INCOMPLETE status. Missing mandatory master data → validation error listing missing items.
Output:  Timetable version ID; quality score (hard-constraint satisfaction rate, soft-constraint score); solver run summary.
Pre-conditions:
▪  Administrator is authenticated.
▪  Academic calendar for the semester is defined.
▪  All required master data (rooms, courses, teachers, sections) is complete.
▪  At least one HARD constraint set exists.
Post-conditions:
▪  A new timetable version was created with status DRAFT.
▪  All class sessions were assigned to rooms and time slots.
▪  Constraint violations (soft) were recorded against the timetable version.
▪  Quality score and solver run log were stored.
OC-06 — UC06 — Review Timetable Quality
Operation:  reviewTimetableQuality(timetableVersionId: String, filters: ReviewFilter [optional])
Use Case:  UC06 — Review Timetable Quality
Actors:  Timetable Administrator, Department Coordinator, Facility Manager
Cross References:  UC05, UC07, UC10
Notes:  Nil
Exceptions:  Timetable version not found → error message. No data available for filter criteria → empty report with advisory.
Output:  Quality report: overall score, hard-violation count (0 expected), soft-violation details, room-utilization statistics, teacher-workload balance metrics.
Pre-conditions:
▪  Actor is authenticated with review permissions.
▪  A timetable version with status DRAFT or PUBLISHED exists for the selected semester.
Post-conditions:
▪  Quality review report was generated and linked to the timetable version.
▪  Soft-constraint violations were listed with severity and affected sessions.
▪  Review event was logged (actor, timestamp, version reviewed).
OC-07 — UC07 — Manually Adjust and Lock Timetable
Operation:  adjustTimetableEntry(sessionId: String, newRoomId: String, newTimeSlot: TimeSlot, lock: Boolean)
Use Case:  UC07 — Manually Adjust and Lock Timetable
Actors:  Timetable Administrator, Department Coordinator
Cross References:  UC06 (Review), UC09 (Change Requests), UC10 (Re-optimize)
Notes:  Nil

Exceptions:  Target slot already occupied → clash error with conflicting session details. Room capacity insufficient for session size → validation error. Session is LOCKED by another actor → edit rejected with lock-owner info.
Output:  Updated session record; recalculated conflict list for the affected slots; new partial quality score.
Pre-conditions:
▪  Actor is authenticated with manual-edit permissions.
▪  The timetable version is in DRAFT or UNDER_REVIEW status.
▪  The target session exists and is not already LOCKED by another actor.
Post-conditions:
▪  Session was moved to the specified room and time slot.
▪  If lock=true, session was marked LOCKED and excluded from future re-optimization.
▪  Constraint scores were recalculated for the affected time slots and rooms.
▪  Audit trail entry was recorded (who moved what and when).
OC-08 — UC08 — Submit Change Request
Operation:  submitChangeRequest(requestData: ChangeRequestDTO)
Use Case:  UC08 — Submit Change Request
Actors:  Teacher, Department Coordinator
Cross References:  UC09 (Approve/Reject), UC07 (Manual Adjust)
Notes:  Nil

Exceptions:  No alternative slot available → system warns actor before submission. Duplicate request for same session → rejected with existing request reference. Request deadline exceeded → form disabled with expiry notice.
Output:  Change request ID; estimated review timeline; current request status (PENDING).
Pre-conditions:
▪  Actor is authenticated as Teacher or Department Coordinator.
▪  The published timetable has a session assigned to the requesting teacher/section.
▪  The request deadline has not passed.
Post-conditions:
▪  A change request record was created with status PENDING.
▪  Notification was sent to the approving authority.
▪  Request was logged in the audit trail.
OC-09 — UC09 — Approve or Reject Change Request
Operation:  processChangeRequest(requestId: String, decision: Enum{APPROVE, REJECT}, notes: String [optional])
Use Case:  UC09 — Approve or Reject Change Request
Actors:  Department Coordinator, Timetable Administrator, Teacher
Cross References:  UC07 (Manual Adjust), UC08 (Submit Request), UC14 (Notifications)
Notes:  Nil

Exceptions:  Requested slot became unavailable between submission and approval → system prompts for alternative before finalizing. Concurrent approval attempt → optimistic lock conflict; second approver sees already-processed status.
Output:  Updated change request record with decision; notification confirmation; updated timetable entry (if APPROVE).
Pre-conditions:
▪  Actor is authenticated with approval permissions.
▪  The change request exists with status PENDING.
▪  For APPROVE: the requested alternative slot is still available.
Post-conditions:
▪  For APPROVE: timetable was updated via adjustTimetableEntry(); request status set to APPROVED.
▪  For REJECT: request status was set to REJECTED; original session unchanged.
▪  Notification was dispatched to the requesting teacher/coordinator.
▪  Decision was recorded in audit log with actor ID and timestamp.
OC-10 — UC10 — Re-optimize Timetable
Operation:  reoptimizeTimetable(timetableVersionId: String, scope: Enum{FULL, PARTIAL}, lockedSessions: List<String>)
Use Case:  UC10 — Re-optimize Timetable
Actors:  Timetable Administrator, Solver Engine
Cross References:  UC05, UC06, UC07
Notes:  Nil

Exceptions:  Solver infeasibility after locking sessions → report returned; no changes committed. Solver timeout → partial result saved; admin notified. No unlocked sessions available → operation rejected with advisory.
Output:  New/updated timetable version ID; quality score delta (before vs. after); list of sessions moved; solver run summary.
Pre-conditions:
▪  Administrator is authenticated.
▪  A timetable version in DRAFT or UNDER_REVIEW status exists.
▪  Solver Engine is available and operational.
Post-conditions:
▪  New timetable version was created (or existing DRAFT updated) with re-optimized assignments.
▪  LOCKED sessions were preserved without modification.
▪  Previous version was archived with status SUPERSEDED.
▪  Re-optimization log and quality delta report were stored.
OC-11 — UC11 — Publish Timetable
Operation:  publishTimetable(timetableVersionId: String, publishScope: Enum{FULL, DEPARTMENTAL})
Use Case:  UC11 — Publish Timetable
Actors:  Timetable Administrator
Cross References:  UC12 (View), UC14 (Notifications), UC13 (Export)
Notes:  Nil
Exceptions:  Remaining HARD constraint violations → publication blocked; violation list returned. Concurrent publish attempt → second attempt rejected with conflict message.
Output:  Publication confirmation with version ID, publish timestamp, and notified-user count.
Pre-conditions:
▪  Administrator is authenticated.
▪  Timetable version has zero HARD constraint violations.
▪  Timetable is in DRAFT or UNDER_REVIEW status (not already PUBLISHED).
Post-conditions:
▪  Timetable version status was set to PUBLISHED.
▪  Previous PUBLISHED version (if any) was archived as HISTORICAL.
▪  All affected users received a publish notification.
▪  Timetable became visible in UC12 view screens.
OC-12 — UC12 — View Personal or Section Timetable
Operation:  viewPersonalTimetable(actorId: String, semesterId: String, viewType: Enum{PERSONAL, SECTION, ROOM})
Use Case:  UC12 — View Personal or Section Timetable
Actors:  Teacher, Student
Cross References:  UC11 (Publish), UC13 (Export)
Notes:  Nil
Exceptions:  No published timetable for semester → informational message returned. Actor not enrolled in any section → empty timetable with advisory.
Output:  Personalized timetable data: sessions filtered by actor role, sorted by day and time.
Pre-conditions:
▪  Actor is authenticated.
▪  A PUBLISHED timetable version exists for the requested semester.
▪  For SECTION view: actor belongs to the requested section.
Post-conditions:
▪  Timetable view event was logged (actor, view type, timestamp).
▪  No data was modified.
OC-13 — UC13 — Export Class Timetable
Operation:  exportTimetable(timetableVersionId: String, format: Enum{PDF, XLSX, CSV, ICS}, scope: Enum{FULL, PERSONAL, DEPARTMENT})
Use Case:  UC13 — Export Class Timetable
Actors:  Timetable Administrator, Teacher, Student
Cross References:  UC11 (Publish), UC12 (View), UC20 (Exam Schedule View)
Notes:  Nil

Exceptions:  Unsupported format requested → error with supported format list. Scope exceeds actor's permissions → export scoped down to permitted data with advisory. File generation timeout → error with retry option.
Output:  Download URL or file stream for the generated export file; file size and expiry timestamp.
Pre-conditions:
▪  Actor is authenticated.
▪  A PUBLISHED timetable version exists.
▪  Actor has permissions matching the requested scope.
Post-conditions:
▪  Export file was generated and stored temporarily in the export cache.
▪  Export event was logged (actor, format, scope, timestamp).
OC-14 — UC14 — Manage Notifications
Operation:  manageNotification(action: Enum{SEND, MARK_READ, DELETE, CONFIG}, notificationData: NotificationDTO)
Use Case:  UC14 — Manage Notifications
Actors:  All Users, System
Cross References:  UC08, UC09, UC11 (event sources that trigger notifications)
Notes:  Nil
Exceptions:  Recipient not found → notification skipped; error logged. Email/push delivery failure → notification retained in inbox; retry queued.
Output:  Notification delivery status; updated inbox summary (unread count).
Pre-conditions:
▪  For SEND: triggering system event has occurred.
▪  For MARK_READ/DELETE: the notification exists and belongs to the actor's inbox.
▪  For CONFIG: actor is authenticated.
Post-conditions:
▪  For SEND: notification record was created in recipient's inbox; push/email dispatched.
▪  For MARK_READ: notification status was updated to READ.
▪  For DELETE: notification was removed from inbox.
▪  For CONFIG: actor's notification preferences were updated.
▪  All actions were logged in the system event log.
OC-15 — UC15 — Compare Timetable Versions
Operation:  compareTimetableVersions(versionIdA: String, versionIdB: String, diffScope: Enum{ALL, DEPARTMENT, ROOM})
Use Case:  UC15 — Compare Timetable Versions
Actors:  Timetable Administrator, Department Coordinator
Cross References:  UC05, UC10, UC11
Notes:  Nil
Exceptions:  Versions from different semesters → rejected with advisory. One or both versions not found → error with version IDs.
Output:  Diff report: list of added, removed, and modified sessions with before/after room-timeslot pairs; summary count by department.
Pre-conditions:
▪  Actor is authenticated with timetable-view permissions.
▪  Both timetable versions exist and belong to the same semester.
Post-conditions:
▪  Diff report was computed and temporarily cached.
▪  Comparison event was logged (actor, versions compared, timestamp).
OC-16 — UC16 — Manage Exam Rooms
Operation:  manageExamRoom(action: Enum{ADD, UPDATE, DELETE, TOGGLE_AVAILABILITY}, examRoomData: ExamRoomDTO)
Use Case:  UC16 — Manage Exam Rooms
Actors:  Timetable Administrator, Facility Manager
Cross References:  UC19 (Schedule Exams), UC03 (Master Data)
Notes:  Nil
Exceptions:  DELETE of room assigned in a scheduled exam → rejected with exam schedule references. Capacity set below minimum allowed value → validation error.
Output:  Updated exam room record with ID, capacity, and current availability status.
Pre-conditions:
▪  Actor is authenticated with exam-room management permissions.
▪  For UPDATE/DELETE/TOGGLE: the exam room record exists.
Post-conditions:
▪  Exam room record was created/updated/deleted/toggled in the exam resource pool.
▪  Room capacity and availability flags were updated.
▪  Change was logged in the audit trail.
OC-17 — UC17 — Manage Student Hierarchy
Operation:  manageStudentHierarchy(action: Enum{ADD, UPDATE, DELETE, IMPORT}, hierarchyData: StudentHierarchyDTO)
Use Case:  UC17 — Manage Student Hierarchy
Actors:  Timetable Administrator
Cross References:  UC18 (Exam Courses), UC19 (Schedule Exams)
Notes:  Nil

Exceptions:  Duplicate student ID or section code → validation error list. Malformed import file → file-level error with line-number details. DELETE of group enrolled in scheduled exam → rejected with dependency list.
Output:  Persisted hierarchy structure; import summary (records created, updated, rejected with reasons).
Pre-conditions:
▪  Administrator is authenticated.
▪  For IMPORT: the import file is in the accepted format (CSV/XLSX) and passes schema validation.
▪  For UPDATE/DELETE: the target group/batch/section record exists.
Post-conditions:
▪  Student grouping hierarchy (batch, program year, section) was persisted.
▪  Student-course enrolment associations were updated.
▪  Import results report was generated for IMPORT action.
▪  Audit log entry was created.
OC-18 — UC18 — Manage Exam Courses
Operation:  manageExamCourse(action: Enum{ADD, UPDATE, DELETE, LINK_STUDENTS}, examCourseData: ExamCourseDTO)
Use Case:  UC18 — Manage Exam Courses
Actors:  Timetable Administrator
Cross References:  UC17 (Student Hierarchy), UC19 (Schedule Exams)
Notes:  Nil
Exceptions:  Duplicate course code in same exam session → validation error. DELETE of course with scheduled exam → rejected with exam reference.
Output:  Exam course record with ID, enrolled student count, and required invigilation ratio.
Pre-conditions:
▪  Administrator is authenticated.
▪  For LINK_STUDENTS: student groups in UC17 are already defined.
▪  For UPDATE/DELETE: the exam course record exists.
Post-conditions:
▪  Exam course (course code, exam duration, eligible student groups) was persisted.
▪  Student-to-exam-course mappings were updated.
▪  Audit log entry was created.
OC-19 — UC19 — Schedule Exams
Operation:  scheduleExams(examSessionId: String, configParams: ExamSolverConfig)
Use Case:  UC19 — Schedule Exams
Actors:  Timetable Administrator, Student, Teacher, Facility Manager
Cross References:  UC16, UC17, UC18, UC20
Notes:  Nil

Exceptions:  Student clash infeasibility → generation fails; clash matrix returned. Insufficient room capacity for enrolment → capacity gap report returned. Solver timeout → partial schedule saved as INCOMPLETE.
Output:  Exam schedule version ID; clash-free confirmation; room-utilization report; invigilation assignment summary.
Pre-conditions:
▪  Administrator is authenticated.
▪  Exam rooms (UC16), student hierarchy (UC17), and exam courses (UC18) are fully configured.
▪  Academic exam window is defined in the academic calendar (UC02).
Post-conditions:
▪  Exam schedule was generated with status DRAFT.
▪  Each exam was assigned a date, time slot, and one or more exam rooms.
▪  Invigilation duties were automatically assigned based on teacher availability.
▪  Clash report (same student in two simultaneous exams) was verified to be zero.
▪  Schedule version and solver log were persisted.
OC-20 — UC20 — View Exam Schedule and Invigilation Duties
Operation:  viewExamSchedule(actorId: String, examSessionId: String, viewType: Enum{STUDENT, TEACHER_INVIGILATION, FACILITY})
Use Case:  UC20 — View Exam Schedule and Invigilation Duties
Actors:  Student, Teacher, Facility Manager
Cross References:  UC19 (Schedule Exams), UC13 (Export)
Notes:  Nil
Exceptions:  No published exam schedule → informational message returned. Actor has no assigned duties/exams → empty view with advisory.
Output:  Filtered exam schedule: for Student — own exam list with dates, rooms, and seat; for Teacher — invigilation duty list; for Facility Manager — room-occupation schedule.
Pre-conditions:
▪  Actor is authenticated.
▪  A PUBLISHED exam schedule exists for the requested exam session.
▪  For TEACHER_INVIGILATION: actor is assigned as an invigilator.
Post-conditions:
▪  View event was logged (actor, view type, timestamp).
▪  No data was modified.
OC-21 — UC21 — Manage Users and Roles
Operation:  manageUserAccount(action: Enum{CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, ASSIGN_ROLE, RESET_PASSWORD}, userData: UserDTO)
Use Case:  UC21 — Manage Users and Roles
Actors:  System Administrator
Cross References:  UC00 (Authentication), UC01 (Organization Hierarchy)
Notes:  Nil
Exceptions:  Duplicate username/email on CREATE → validation error. DELETE of the only ADMIN account → rejected with safety message. Unauthorized role assignment → rejected.
Output:  Updated user record; action confirmation with timestamp; for RESET_PASSWORD: masked delivery confirmation.
Pre-conditions:
▪  System Administrator is authenticated.
▪  For UPDATE/DELETE/ASSIGN_ROLE/RESET_PASSWORD: the target user account exists.
▪  For ASSIGN_ROLE: the role being assigned is defined in the system role catalogue.
Post-conditions:
▪  For CREATE: new user account was persisted with hashed credentials; welcome email sent.
▪  For UPDATE: user profile attributes were updated.
▪  For DELETE: account was deactivated (soft-delete); active sessions invalidated.
▪  For ASSIGN_ROLE: role-permission mapping was updated; effective immediately.
▪  For RESET_PASSWORD: temporary password was generated; reset link emailed.
▪  All actions were logged in security audit trail.
OC-22 — UC22 — View System Reports and Audit Logs
Operation:  viewSystemReports(reportType: Enum{AUDIT_LOG, USAGE, SOLVER_PERFORMANCE, ROOM_UTILIZATION, ...}, filterParams: ReportFilter)
Use Case:  UC22 — View System Reports and Audit Logs
Actors:  Timetable Administrator, Facility Manager, System Administrator, Department Coordinator
Cross References:  All UCs (audit log aggregates events from every use case)
Notes:  Nil

Exceptions:  Filter yields no results → empty report with advisory. Actor requests report type outside their permissions → access denied with permitted report list. Report generation exceeds timeout → partial result with truncation notice.
Output:  Report data: tabular rows or aggregated metrics matching the filter; export option (PDF/XLSX) URL.
Pre-conditions:
▪  Actor is authenticated with reporting permissions appropriate to the requested report type.
▪  The date range and filter parameters are valid.
Post-conditions:
▪  Report was generated from the audit and metrics database.
▪  Report-access event was logged (actor, report type, filter, timestamp).
